rag_rules: Fix type mismatches in AGREE expressions by ensuring left and right sides of binary operations are of the same type.
Resolve 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by tracing unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations on the same line.
Correct syntax errors such as unexpected tokens or missing semicolons in AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, ensure all expressions are type-consistent and all named things are valid expressions with defined types.
When encountering AGREE validation errors, trace the root cause to unresolved references or invalid declarations rather than adding new architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex for AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Binary expressions in AGREE require operands of matching types.
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE expressions are either visible AADL features or previously declared in the same annex.
anti_patterns: Avoid adding new architecture elements to resolve AGREE type or naming errors; instead, fix the root cause in the existing contract.
Do not attempt to correct type mismatches by casting or implicit conversion without understanding the underlying AADL type system.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes vs implementation annexes
AGREE validator diagnostics for unrelated components
